zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaretha van Holland於1234年出生在荷蘭布拉班特時，她的父親Floris IV van Holland24歲，母親Mechteld van Brabant35歲。她於從1249年1月8日到1250年1月7日與Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1277年3月26日在荷蘭-拉藍德丹 哈格享年43歲，埋葬在荷蘭-拉藍德丹 哈格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaretha van Holland于1234年出生在荷兰布拉班特时，她的父亲Floris IV van Holland24岁，母亲Mechteld van Brabant35岁。她于从1249年1月8日到1250年1月7日与Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1277年3月26日在荷兰-拉藍德丹 哈格享年43岁，埋葬在荷兰-拉藍德丹 哈格。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaretha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margarethe
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaretha van Holland was born in 1234, in Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Floris IV van Holland, was 24 and her mother, Mechteld van Brabant, was 35. She married Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg from 8 January 1249 to 7 January 1250. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 26 March 1277, in The Hague, South Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 43, and was buried in The Hague, South Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaretha van Holland est née en 1234, à Brabant, Pays-Bas, son père, Floris IV van Holland, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Mechteld van Brabant, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg du 8 janvier 1249 au 7 janvier 1250. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 26 mars 1277, à La Haye, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterrée à La Haye, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaretha van Holland 1234 in Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Floris IV van Holland 24 und ihre Mutter Mechteld van Brabant 35. Sie hat von 8. Januar 1249 bis 7. Januar 1250 Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 26. März 1277 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Den Haag, Südholland, Niederlande und wurde in Den Haag, Südholland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaretha van Holland è nata il nel 1234 nel Brabant, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Floris IV van Holland, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Mechteld van Brabant, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg da 8 gennaio 1249 a 7 gennaio 1250. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 26 marzo 1277, nel Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolta a Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaretha van Hollandが1234年にオランダブラバントで生まれた時，父Floris IV van Hollandは24歳，母Mechteld van Brabantは35歳であった。から1249年1月8日まで1250年1月7日にHermann I. Graf von Hennebergと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1277年3月26日にオランダズイド-hオランドデン ハアグで43歳で死去し，オランダズイド-hオランドデン ハアグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaretha van Holland(이)가 1234년 네덜란드 브라반트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Floris IV van Holland(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mechteld van Brabant(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1249년 1월 8일부터 1250년 1월 7일까지 Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1277년 3월 26일 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 든 하아그에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 든 하아그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaretha van Holland nasceu em 1234, em Brabant, Países Baixos, seu pai, Floris IV van Holland, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Mechteld van Brabant, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg de 8 de janeiro de 1249 a 7 de janeiro de 1250. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 26 de março de 1277, em Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, com 43 anos, e foi sepultada em Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaretha van Holland родилась в 1234 г. в местности брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Floris IV van Holland было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Mechteld van Brabant, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg с 8 января 1249 по 7 января 1250. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 26 марта 1277 г. в местности ден хааг, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности ден хааг, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaretha van Holland nació en 1234, en Brabant, Países Bajos, su papá, Floris IV van Holland, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Mechteld van Brabant, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg desde 8 de enero de 1249 hasta 7 de enero de 1250. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 26 de marzo de 1277, en Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultada en Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos.
